question_text: A student compares three objects at different heights on a shelf. Object A is heavy and high up, Object B is light and high up, and Object C is heavy and low down. Which object has the most gravitational potential energy stored, and explain why using the factors that affect gravitational potential energy.
correct_answer: Object A has the most gravitational potential energy because it has both large mass and large height. Gravitational potential energy depends on both factors: the heavier an object and the higher it is, the more potential energy it stores.
explanation: Gravitational potential energy is determined by two factors: mass and height. Object A scores highest on both—it is the heaviest and the highest. Although Object B is also high, it is light, so it stores less energy. Object C is heavy but low down. The formula is GPE = mass × gravity × height, so both mass and height are crucial.
